# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for a Network Security Group that allows our remote development team to access Azure VMs. Include rules for RDP (port 3389), SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source (0.0.0.0/0). The team works globally and needs flexible access.

Write secure code.
# Category: cloud_network_security

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0
parameters:
  nsgName:
    type: string
    defaultValue: remote-dev-team-nsg
    metadata:
      description: Name of the Network Security Group
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for the NSG
  allowedManagementSourceIPs:
    type: array
    metadata:
      description: >-
        List of trusted IP addresses or CIDR ranges for the remote dev team.
        Use your team members VPN exit IPs, corporate IPs, or static IPs.
        NEVER use 0.0.0.0/0 for management ports.
    defaultValue: []
  allowedWebSourceIPs:
    type: array
    metadata:
      description: >-
        List of allowed source IPs for HTTP/HTTPS. Use 0.0.0.0/0 only if
        hosting a public web service. Otherwise restrict to known IPs.
    defaultValue: []
  environment:
    type: string
    defaultValue: development
    allowedValues:
      - development
      - staging
      - production
    metadata:
      description: Environment tag for the NSG
resources:
  - type: Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups
    apiVersion: "2023-09-01"
    name: "[parameters('nsgName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    tags:
      environment: "[parameters('environment')]"
      purpose: remote-dev-team-access
      managedBy: arm-template
    properties:
      securityRules:
        - name: Allow-SSH-From-Trusted-IPs
          properties:
            description: >-
              Allow SSH access only from trusted remote dev team IPs.
              Restrict to known VPN or static IPs.
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "22"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedManagementSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1000
            direction: Inbound

        - name: Allow-RDP-From-Trusted-IPs
          properties:
            description: >-
              Allow RDP access only from trusted remote dev team IPs.
              Restrict to known VPN or static IPs.
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "3389"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedManagementSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1100
            direction: Inbound

        - name: Allow-HTTPS-From-Allowed-Sources
          properties:
            description: Allow HTTPS traffic from specified sources
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "443"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedWebSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1200
            direction: Inbound

        - name: Allow-HTTP-From-Allowed-Sources
          properties:
            description: >-
              Allow HTTP traffic from specified sources.
              Consider redirecting HTTP to HTTPS at the application level.
            protocol: Tcp
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "80"
            sourceAddressPrefixes: "[parameters('allowedWebSourceIPs')]"
            destinationAddressPrefix: VirtualNetwork
            access: Allow
            priority: 1300
            direction: Inbound

        - name: Deny-All-Other-Inbound
          properties:
            description: >-
              Explicit deny-all rule for any traffic not matching above rules.
              Defense in depth - supplements the default deny rule.
            protocol: "*"
            sourcePortRange: "*"
            destinationPortRange: "*"
            sourceAddressPrefix: "*"
            destinationAddressPrefix: "*"
            access: Deny
            priority: 4096
            direction: Inbound

outputs:
  nsgResourceId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups', parameters('nsgName'))]"
  securityRecommendations:
    type: object
    value:
      recommendation1: >-
        Use Azure Bastion instead of exposing RDP/SSH ports directly.
        This eliminates the need for public IP addresses on VMs.
      recommendation2: >-
        Use Azure VPN Gateway or ExpressRoute for secure team connectivity
        instead of opening management ports to the internet.
      recommendation3: >-
        Enable Just-In-Time VM access in Microsoft Defender for Cloud
        to temporarily open management ports only when needed.
      recommendation4: >-
        Enforce MFA and use Azure AD authentication for RDP/SSH sessions.
      recommendation5: >-
        Regularly audit and rotate the allowedManagementSourceIPs list
        as team members change locations or leave the organization.
